beats.narration: Visual testing gets real when you put a number on what you see. Acceptance criteria are numbers — and numbers need the right tool.

payload.title: THE 10:1 RULE
payload.sub: Match the tool to the tolerance before you trust the number.

beats.narration: Here's the habit: pick a tool whose resolution is about one tenth of the tolerance. Many calibration programs accept four-to-one as the floor.
Here's why. A tool as coarse as the tolerance band lets uncertainty swallow the accept-or-reject call. Ten times finer makes the verdict clear.
The toolkit steps down. A steel rule resolves one sixty-fourth of an inch, a caliper one thousandth, a micrometer with vernier one ten-thousandth.
Lock in the habit. Ten times finer than the tolerance — or the number you write down is a guess.

payload.sub: Resolution one tenth of the tolerance, every time.
